entries.definition: eager or enthusiastic about something or to do something
entries.exampleSentence: She's a keen student who always sits in the front row.

entries.definition: highly developed; sharp (of senses or mental ability)
entries.exampleSentence: Hawks have keen eyesight that allows them to spot prey from great distances.

entries.definition: intense or strong (of feelings, competition, etc.)
entries.exampleSentence: There was keen competition for the scholarship, with over 500 applicants.

entries.definition: very low or competitive (of prices, especially in British English)
entries.exampleSentence: The shop offers keen prices on all its electronics.
